mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates a robust economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and thus drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon as investors price in future growth.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth rate is positive but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains persistently high, it signals ongoing inflationary pressures, prompting the Federal Reserve to maintain a hawkish stance, which negatively impacts equity valuations by increasing discount rates and reducing future earnings expectations.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL index value exceeds 320.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a tight labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and potential central bank tightening, leading to negative short-term equity market reactions as profit margins are squeezed.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when both PAYEMS growth and UNRATE conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) during periods of moderate economic growth signal strong consumer confidence and spending, which directly supports corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. This effect is amplified when growth is not overheating.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5% during moderate growth regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained low unemployment rate (UNRATE) reflects a healthy and productive economy, which underpins consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to a generally positive outlook for equity markets over a longer investment horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with UNRATE levels below 5% over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) crosses a critical threshold, it signals a shift in monetary policy stance. A rate above 4.0% indicates a restrictive policy, increasing borrowing costs and reducing liquidity, which negatively impacts equity valuations in the short term.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad-based slowdown in supply-side activity and investment, signaling weakening economic momentum that negatively impacts corporate earnings and thus S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average change < -0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average change < -5.0%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both INDPRO and HOUST show significant declines.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts (HOUST) during periods of low interest rates indicates strong housing demand and investment, which has a broad stimulative effect on the economy and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where HOUST shows strong growth and FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%.
